page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-96GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 16 shared vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7542 processors running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively low, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance falling into the bottom 10% and single-threaded bzip3 decompression rated as weak. However, the 16 physical cores deliver average multi-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests, alongside strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth and database operations consistently perform within the average range.

Utilizing a shared CPU allocation model, this server offers a cost-effective profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated, continuous CPU execution. The main tradeoff is the shared nature of the vCPUs, which can lead to performance variability under sustained single-core loads, as reflected in the lower single-core benchmark scores.

This configuration is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, memory-cached databases, static web serving, and moderate parallel processing tasks. It is less appropriate for single-threaded computations or real-time LLM text generation due to its lower single-core throughput.
description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-96GB is an x86_64 virtual server configured with 16 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz and 96.0 GB of RAM. Running on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU resources. Benchmarks reveal average multi-core and memory performance, highlighted by strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, contrasted against poor single-core CPU performance. Due to its shared CPU allocation, the server provides a cost-efficient option for memory-heavy workloads that can tolerate variable CPU performance. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and database operations rather than single-threaded or high-compute tasks.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-96GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 96 GB of RAM, and no local storage, offering balanced multi-core performance for memory-reliant workloads.

tagline: A shared-CPU virtual server delivering ninety-six gigabytes of memory and sixteen cores for cost-effective multi-threaded workloads.

96.0 GB of system memory with 6.0 GB per core
